name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: This isn't a typical House race
body:
| [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55b0/24c69fd9/11e31e309/189449f6/810256195/VEsH/ "https://www.emilyslist.org/donate?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFCN14N_digital_email_20180208_MarieNewman_inact&source=WQEFCN14N_digital_email_20180208_MarieNewman_inact")
---

Peter,

For years, Republicans have _relentlessly_ attacked choice in Congress -- and they're only more emboldened now that President Trump sits in the Oval Office.

That's why it's critical we elect pro-choice champions in November who will fight back against these attacks and defend choice.

We've endorsed Marie Newman in Illinois' 3rd Congressional District to do just that. Marie is a small business owner and a nonprofit executive with a strong record on reproductive rights. Marie is challenging Rep. Dan Lipinski, a conservative anti-choice Democrat whose beliefs are woefully mismatched with the community he represents.

**[Donate $3 to Marie Newman today and 100% of your contribution will go directly to her campaign.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55b0/24c69fd9/11e31e309/189449f7/810256195/VEsE/ "https://emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/marie-newman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+Marie+Newman+today+and+100+o&utm_campaign=WQEFCN14N_digital_email_20180208_MarieNewman_inact&source=WQEFCN14N_digital_email_20180208_MarieNewman_inact")**

Dan Lipinski is a longtime opponent of reproductive rights -- he's voted repeatedly for dangerous abortion bans, to defund Planned Parenthood, and against Obamacare.

He even supported a constitutional amendment to ban same-sex marriage and co-sponsored legislation that would allow businesses to discriminate against LGBTQ people.

At a time when Republicans are attacking our rights at every turn, we need a fighter in Congress who will actually _defend_ women's reproductive rights.

That's why we've joined with a coalition of pro-choice organizations -- including NARAL Pro-Choice America and Planned Parenthood Action Fund -- to defeat Lipinski. The stakes are too high for women.

**Donate $3 to Marie Newman right now and 100% of your contribution will go straight to her campaign:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Support-Marie-Newman](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c55b0/24c69fd9/11e31e309/189449f0/810256195/VEsF/ "https://emilyslist.org/donate/candidate/marie-newman?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgSupportMarieNewm&utm_campaign=WQEFCN14N_digital_email_20180208_MarieNewman_inact&source=WQEFCN14N_digital_email_20180208_MarieNewman_inact")**

Thank you,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

name: Patrick
subject: 33 Days - Campaign Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rick for Congress
body:
With 33 days until March 13th, the race is heating up!

There are 33 days until March 13th, find out how to get involved!

DEPLOYING RICK'S GROUND CREW

This Saturday, Rick’s Ground Crew will be out in full force! We’ll be going door to door, making phone calls, and have a large push for distributing yard signs!

>From 10:00 am to 3:00 pm each of our county headquarters will be up and running!

Washington: 135 Technology Drive, Suite 204, Canonsburg, PA 15317

Allegheny: 100 Fleet Street, Suite 205, Pittsburgh, PA 15220

Westmoreland: 23 N. Maple Ave, Greensburg, PA 15601

Light refreshments will be provided to all who attend! Let’s make a major push this weekend to get Rick Saccone elected to Congress!

4x8 YARD SIGNS

We'll be getting a new shipment of 4x8 yard signs! These are reserved for highly visible locations that are preferably on private property. If you'd like a sign, please email sacconesocialmedia@gmail.com with your contact information and the location of where you'd like to place a sign.

ABSENTEE BALLOTS

If you will not be in the district on election day and would still like to vote, please fill out an absentee ballot form at the following link: http://www.votespa.com/en-us.

VOLUNTEERING AT HOME

For those who cannot make it into the campaign offices, and would still like to be involved, you can make phone calls from your home with the new phone banking app. For more information, please contact our State GOP Coordinators:

[Allegheny] Robert Byers – Rbyers@pagop.org – (814) 853-6257

[Westmoreland] Emily Greene – Egreene@pagop.org – (412) 302-3338

[Washington & Greene] Bobby Johnson - bjohnson@pagop.org - (757) 266-1425

YARD SIGNS

We've just got a new order of yard signs on Monday!

We've set up yard sign distribution hubs in many municipalities. For information on a hub close to you, please call the campaign HQ at (724) 655-3400.

If you’d wish to support Rick Saccone from your yard, signs are available at all the county headquarters. Their addresses are here:

Allegheny: 100 Fleet Street, Suite 205, Pittsburgh, PA 15220

Westmoreland: 23 N Maple Ave, Greensburg PA 15601

Washington: 135 Technology Drive, Suite 204, Canonsburg, PA 15317

Greene: 52 E. High Street, Waynesburg, PA 15370

PETITIONS

The Pennsylvania Department of State says it will make nomination petition forms and instructions available for the office of Representative in Congress as soon as possible after a new Congressional Redistricting Plan is approved [http://www.dos.pa.gov/VotingElections/CandidatesCommittees/RunningforOffice/Pages/Petition-Notice.aspx].

Let us work together to get Rick Saccone Elected to Congress!

~The Saccone Campaign

Paid for by Rick for Congress

Unsubscribe http://ricksaccone.cmail20.com/t/j-u-bnlhhy-ykkrijcdu-b/

name: The White House
subject: President Trump delivers huge win for military
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=568cf2aef0fab525ff1c1d6b1363d3a7340c99fa156e7ab993cd25017c7535f3edd6baa959d447a3e515d1746f57632595d828c9ce430f12

President Trump delivers huge win for military

President Donald J. Trump signed a two-year, bipartisan budget bill this morning that ends Congress’ brief overnight shutdown of the federal government.

“Just signed Bill. Our Military will now be stronger than ever before,” President Trump tweeted this morning. “We love and need our Military and gave them everything — and more. First time this has happened in a long time.”

The President then laid out what must be accomplished in 2018. “Costs on non-military lines will never come down if we do not elect more Republicans in the 2018 Election, and beyond,” he tweeted. “This Bill is a BIG VICTORY for our Military, but much waste in order to get Dem votes.”

Read more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=568cf2aef0fab5258c8454721d12c2c6a43f8b362614637db196a28ad74d82bc7aafef1a57be6fc08bf81cf91b47d6697f4eec1201ac029d
Press Secretary Sarah Sanders and Defense Secretary James Mattis explain the budget deal.

‘A magnificent Winter Olympics’

The 2018 Winter Olympic Games in PyeongChang, South Korea, kicked off in spectacular fashion with its Opening Ceremony early this morning. Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence are in South Korea to lead the American Olympic delegation.

The Vice President held a bilateral meeting with South Korean President Moon Jae-in in Seoul yesterday before the Games. “Allow me to join President Trump in congratulating South Korea on what we know, in his words, ‘will be a magnificent Winter Olympics,’” Vice President Pence said.

As U.S. athletes compete in the Games, America will remain vigilant against threats to national security. “We’ll be there to cheer on our American athletes, but we’ll also be there to stand with our allies and remind the world that North Korea is the most tyrannical and oppressive regime on the planet,” the Vice President said Wednesday.
“The United States of America will continue to stand shoulder-to-shoulder [with South Koreans] in our effort to bring maximum pressure to bear on North Korea,” he told President Moon yesterday.

Watch:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=568cf2aef0fab525ea0c3f9836ef0d707fd9dbb5a5862a27dac19431f7701d698d0d4d85a43a60b70d389680cacd4717313dcac08efa026a
Vice President Pence visits Japan earlier this week

The economic harm caused by our immigration system

As President Trump applauds congressional leaders for their budget deal that funds our great military for two years, he and Americans turn their sights to the pressing need for immigration reform in the weeks ahead.

“It is time to create a merit-based immigration system that makes sense for a modern economy,” the President has said. Here’s what he means:

- Low-skilled labor: Low-skilled immigration has put downward pressure on U.S. wages at the expense of vulnerable American workers. Real hourly wages for Americans with a high school education or less are lower today than in 1979.
- A strain on public resources: A majority, 51 percent, of immigrant-headed households uses at least one welfare program.
- Reform to benefit the economy: President Trump’s immigration framework would end the visa lottery program and help reduce the backlog of high-skilled, employment-based immigrant cases.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=568cf2aef0fab52528c3ff9feb0ecc3c9017b83524f2c5831dea9c1d6c47e5078976ec1c9eb3b1b2e50ba29ecd9e22f0583922a9286e95c9
Read more about how America’s immigration system prioritizes all the wrong things.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump delivers remarks at the National Prayer Breakfast | February 8, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump will receive an intelligence briefing and later meet with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.

In the afternoon, the President will meet with EPA Administrator Scott Pruitt.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=568cf2aef0fab5258673df9ade424cf749f3d52c4e8c3db3dc0bd1b80d34892be7c90bfcc9cda26a1fac34bcffadcefe477a66035a9562bc
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=568cf2aef0fab525d90f3ab788f1f2a93acf557d99c786f43c00a5af34ec77e4cd542fb573e50c387f1b42d72ac0f05ac2720d4816e2a6bb
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4e339699a4e18c4812ef0585b04303f802498366e5b88191bcbeff413ae16b1f0dc28c02add900a2e135ad77e28b031c967225265dc31d3c5
Unsubscribe

name: Megan West
subject: Exclusive Video Update from Congressman Arrington
body:
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180209com01&n=1)
Peter,
On Wednesday I was privileged to sit down with Congressman Jodey Arrington of Texas in Washington D.C. on a My Faith Votes Facebook Live.
It's unlike anything you will hear on the major cable television networks.
Congressman Arrington makes clear his faith is not found in government. His Christian perspective on his job in Washington D.C. and his message to My Faith Votes followers is refreshing!
[If you missed the interview you can watch it now!](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FNmX1YwRC4SQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180209com01&n=2)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fyoutu.be%2FNmX1YwRC4SQ&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180209com01&n=3)
The Congressman also provided us a brief update on the budget deal and the debate in Washington regarding DACA and keeping the military funded.
Peter, watch and share the interview with your friends and family. Christian leaders like Congressman Arrington are a great encouragement and far too often this Christian perspective doesn't get the platform it deserves.
**United. We Stand.**
Megan West
My Faith Votes
©2018 My Faith Votes
10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

subject: Week 5 Legislative Session Update
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | |
---|---
|
Dear Peter,

Business in the Virginia Senate continues at a diligent pace with two months to complete this year's business. So far, we have acted on 775 bills, passing 313 and laying the groundwork for many important proposals we will study over the summer and fall.

My Bills
Autism Insurance Coverage: Last week, my bill extending health insurance coverage for children with autism passed unanimously out of its final committee. When I first started working on this issue in 2009, it seemed as if the weight of opposition made this insurmountable. Finally, after years of work we secured coverage for diagnosis and treatment of autism through age 10. This year, I am hopeful that we finally extend this life changing coverage to age 18.
Fighting Opioid Abuse: Tackling Virginia's opioid epidemic is one of my priorities this session. My first bill on the subject required pharmacies to take back unused prescription medications, while the other helped crack down on "pill mills" through better wholesale supply chain tracking. Just this week, it was reported that 20.8 million opioid pills were shipped to one town of 2900 people in West Virginia. We have to do more to address this issue and I am proud that we made progress on both bills and look forward to further study in the year ahead, as we work with all stakeholders on getting both life-saving bills passed into law. This will help curb misuse while also protecting legitimate patients who need their medication.
Strengthening Ethics: Last week I also presented my bill prohibiting candidates from spending campaign funds on personal expenses. Unfortunately, it did not pass in committee. I am sorry to see this ethics measure fail but I will continue working until we join the 47 other states which prohibit the practice. [Read News 8's coverage of this issue here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001y3qgYl7auhg_GKL0RRNeppHaqpjUH9DuEXFj5RpalCP_4b7GsaC5w4e-zyfUA1NuP5GCoizTgQ2VxQsUq-q5oWaaPg-VmaN6oWsw_fpyo8_A9UUcVeS4JQxBOtjZY9bKOo5spMym-wtfUveSxhUfv5AI62bG6UuPXz52LNdVkcqcLYyxaJhCo7F-227I1Sg5hzdJJhmCLMtQsIueWPIbGXdTnIOyscMPNOtkPLeOxoWlWf29-mK8Zg==&c=Wrf2xbgHJRdKsth2LKZW5jVyB-flFKqiOCrq7uvrPeQnrgOHym3FiQ==&ch=Y6__zsXdvmbHSutlosHAbM2VuzxI93Jh3B2FzyHTTyKWJ-lP-WHqSA==).

A New Addiction Treatment Facility: This week I successfully advanced legislation which would transfer surplus state property to Clarke County. Among the priorities in Clarke County has been to support a growing need in the region for addiction treatment and recovery services and the hope is to use a portion of the property to support that project.

Working to Lower Health Care Costs: Also this week I presented a bill to increase competition and choice in health care by expanding options for MRI and CT scans in our community. As health plan changes classify fewer imaging centers as in-network and healthcare options generally become less affordable, my constituents continue to request measures to create more competition and better choices. The bill was carried over for the year for further study.

Risk-Limiting Post-Election Audits: I advanced a bill out of committee this week to provide for post-election audits which verify the accuracy of reported vote totals and proper functioning of election equipment. After working with registrars, technical experts and non-partisan advocacy groups, I am pleased that we have agreement on legislation creating a panel of experts to develop the policies and statistical procedures we need for this important goal.
| | |
---|---
|
Virginia's Budget & Other Issues
Our work continues on other important issues, from relieving congestion and improving safety on I-81, to ensuring we protect agriculture and our environment. In response to your concerns, we have also debated legislation addressing the exorbitant tolls on I-66.

In the Senate Finance Committee on which I serve, we continue to work to construct a balanced and fiscally responsible budget without tax hikes. Billions of dollars are at stake and there are 508 separate budget amendments to be heard. We will examine every dollar in the budget to ensure taxpayer money is spent wisely and on our highest priorities.

For our district, I am working hard to secure funding for a new career and technical education center, a new local barracks for our state police, and additional resources to protect survivors of domestic violence among other measures.

While public attention often focuses on high profile issues which generate the most headlines, we work on bills dealing with everything from occupational licensing, to tourism promotion, to fire safety. Whether considering big issues like our budget or transportation funding, to lesser-known issues like adoption leave benefits, we have two months to get these bills right. That's why we spend most of our time hearing bills in committee and meeting with stakeholders and representatives from home wishing to share their input.
| | |
---|---
|
I urge you to contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached during the General Assembly session at 804-698-7527 or by email at [district27@senate.virginia.gov](mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov). Our office appreciates the opportunity to serve and we are very grateful for your continued support.

Sincerely,
|

| Senator Jill Vogel

Just this week, it was reported that 20.8 million opioid pills were shipped to one town of 2900 people in West Virginia. We have to do more to address this issue and I am proud that we made progress on both bills and look forward to further study in the year ahead, as we work with all stakeholders on getting both life-saving bills passed into law. This will help curb misuse while also protecting legitimate patients who need their medication. Strengthening Ethics: Last week I also presented my bill prohibiting candidates from spending campaign funds on personal expenses. Unfortunately, it did not pass in committee. I am sorry to see this ethics measure fail but I will continue working until we join the 47 other states which prohibit the practice. ==. A New Addiction Treatment Facility: This week I successfully advanced legislation which would transfer surplus state property to Clarke County. Among the priorities in Clarke County has been to support a growing need in the region for addiction treatment and recovery services and the hope is to use a portion of the property to support that project. Working to Lower Health Care Costs: Also this week I presented a bill to increase competition and choice in health care by expanding options for MRI and CT scans in our community. As health plan changes classify fewer imaging centers as in-network and healthcare options generally become less affordable, my constituents continue to request measures to create more competition and better choices. The bill was carried over for the year for further study. Risk-Limiting Post-Election Audits: I advanced a bill out of committee this week to provide for post-election audits which verify the accuracy of reported vote totals and proper functioning of election equipment. After working with registrars, technical experts and non-partisan advocacy groups, I am pleased that we have agreement on legislation creating a panel of experts to develop the policies and statistical procedures we need for this important goal. | | | ---|--- | Virginia's Budget & Other Issues Our work continues on other important issues, from relieving congestion and improving safety on I-81, to ensuring we protect agriculture and our environment. In response to your concerns, we have also debated legislation addressing the exorbitant tolls on I-66. In the Senate Finance Committee on which I serve, we continue to work to construct a balanced and fiscally responsible budget without tax hikes. Billions of dollars are at stake and there are 508 separate budget amendments to be heard. We will examine every dollar in the budget to ensure taxpayer money is spent wisely and on our highest priorities. For our district, I am working hard to secure funding for a new career and technical education center, a new local barracks for our state police, and additional resources to protect survivors of domestic violence among other measures. While public attention often focuses on high profile issues which generate the most headlines, we work on bills dealing with everything from occupational licensing, to tourism promotion, to fire safety. Whether considering big issues like our budget or transportation funding, to lesser-known issues like adoption leave benefits, we have two months to get these bills right. That's why we spend most of our time hearing bills in committee and meeting with stakeholders and representatives from home wishing to share their input. | | | ---|--- | I urge you to contact our office any time that you have questions or concerns. I can be reached during the General Assembly session at 804-698-7527 or by email at mailto:district27@senate.virginia.gov. Our office appreciates the opportunity to serve and we are very grateful for your continued support. Sincerely, | | Senator Jill Vogel | | | Paid for and Authorized by Vogel for Senate45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100Warrenton, VA 20186 | Follow Jill on Facebook and Twitter for Campaign Updates == == | | Vogel for Virginia | 45 North Hill Drive, Suite 100, Warrenton, VA 20186 | | Sent by mailto:info@senatorjillvogel.com in collaboration with |

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: One year later, add your name to keep persisting with me in this fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
One year after the GOP tried to silence me, I'm still persisting in our fight for the America we believe in. But I can't do it without your support, team.
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d538/2b07114c/8ec53848/731cf78c/3004071374/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/in-for-2018?source=PT-20180209-Warren-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
Team --

Last February, I was giving a speech on the Senate floor, opposing Jeff Sessions' nomination as Attorney General, and I read a letter from Coretta Scott King.

Mitch McConnell interrupted my speech and told me to stop talking. When I objected, he said:

_**" She was warned. She was given an explanation. Nevertheless, she persisted."**_
You bet I did. And one year later, we're still persisting in our fight for the America we believe in. And we hope that grassroots Democrats like you are still with us, team.

**That 's why I'm asking you to join the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee in re-electing Senate Democrats like me and winning back the Senate majority.**

[**Can I count on you, team? Add your name right now to commit to fighting for the Senate majority.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d538/2b07114c/8ec53848/731cf78c/3004071374/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/in-for-2018?source=PT-20180209-Warren-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

If we can flip two more Senate seats -- just _two_ seats -- we can take back the Senate and build a Democratic firewall against all of Donald Trump's harmful policies. That's why the DSCC is ramping up all its efforts to elect strong challengers like Jacky Rosen and re-elect our Democratic Senate champions like Tammy Baldwin and Debbie Stabenow (and yes, me too!).

The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to electing Senate Democrats. They were there for me when I launched my Senate campaign in Massachusetts, and they were there to help candidates like Catherine Cortez Masto and Tammy Duckworth launch their successful 2016 campaigns, too.

And with your help, the DSCC can be there for each of our 2018 candidates -- making sure they have the support they need at every step of the way to flip the Senate.

[**Add your name right now to commit to supporting all our Democratic Senate candidates and fighting for the Senate majority.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d538/2b07114c/8ec53848/731cf78c/3004071374/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/in-for-2018?source=PT-20180209-Warren-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you for stepping up,

Elizabeth Warren

subject: This Energy is Contagious!
body:
Last week, I had the honor of representing Indiana at the Republican National Committee Winter Meeting in Washington, D.C. Alongside our... | | |

| **_This Energy is Contagious!_** Last week, I had the honor of representing Indiana at the Republican National Committee Winter Meeting in Washington, D.C.

Alongside our**National Committeeman John Hammond** and our outstanding team of executive directors here at your Indiana Republican Party, we heard directly from **President Donald Trump** and **Vice President Mike Penc** e – and their message was clear: Full steam ahead!

One year into the President Trump’s tenue, he is energized to continue the momentum generated by rolling back regulations (eliminating 22 existing regulations for every one new regulation) and cutting federal taxes for hardworking Americans.

That same energy translated to the delegations from every state in the nation at the meeting! Across the country, Republicans are already hard at work to promote our candidates and share our message for 2018. The energy is contagious!

And when it to comes to the 49 other states in the nation, we had a message for them: In order to protect the United States Senate, Indiana will be here to ignite a firewall. By defeating Democrat Joe Donnelly and electing a strong conservative Republican to be Indiana’s next senator, we’ll make sure the U.S. Senate remains in Republican hands!

As you’re attending your local Lincoln Day Dinner this spring ([check the calendar if you don’t know your local date](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ca3405c3b5&e=1acd9bb3d6)), I hope you catch the same contagious energy we felt at the RNC Winter Meeting. Big races (and big wins!) are ahead for us in 2018!

-Kyle Hupfer

_Picture: Chairman Kyle Hupfer and Governor Eric Holcomb pose for a photo with Vietnam War veteran and Medal of Honor recipient Sammy Davis and Iraq War veteran and Purple Heart recipient Josh Bleill. Before the Super Bowl last week, Sammy and other Medal of Honor recipients led the coin toss for the big game._

| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=28e7c946a7&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Legislative Session ****Reaches**** Midway Point**_ This week marked the halfway point of the 2018 legislative session. All of **Governor Eric Holcomb's** [major legislative priorities](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9b2ae7dcf1&e=1acd9bb3d6) are continuing to move forward through the legislative process. These bills each fit into his five pillars:
* Cultivate a strong and diverse economy
* Maintain and build the state's infrastructure
* Develop a 21st century skilled and ready workforce
* Attack the drug epidemic
* Deliver great government service

And speaking of Governor Holcomb's five pillars, two major headlines this week are helping advance the cause! This includes an update to the [Next Level Recovery website](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=49df4ad990&e=1acd9bb3d6), providing a new tool that makes it easier for Hoosiers to find Division of Mental Health and Addiction-certified treatment providers throughout the state.

Additionally -- and this is exciting -- [Indiana is one step closer to having a state insect](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=bf6ab64b70&e=1acd9bb3d6)! Governor Holcomb has teamed-up with students from across Indiana to make the Say's Firefly the official state insect. With the support of these determined students, the bill has passed the Indiana Senate, and now moves to the House!

We've got it all! Whether you've always wanted your very own Make America Great Again hat, Holcomb pull-over sweater or Indiana GOP coffee mug, we've got the goods for you! Support your Indiana Republican Party [by purchasing new gear today](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8b9ad859a9&e=1acd9bb3d6).
